# Portfolio Site Implementation Plan

> **For Claude:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers-extended-cc:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.

**Goal:** Build and deploy Sam's personal portfolio site — a minimal Astro + Tailwind + MDX static site with curated project cards, featured case-study detail pages, and outbound links to Medium, GitHub, and TestFlight.

**Architecture:** Static site generated by Astro. Content collections back a `projects` collection (Markdown + MDX) with per-project frontmatter controlling visibility, ordering, and card metadata. Featured projects additionally render MDX bodies on `/projects/[slug]` detail pages. Tailwind supplies design tokens and utility classes. Deploy via Vercel with GitHub auto-deploy on push to `main`.

**Tech Stack:** Astro 4.x, Tailwind CSS, MDX, TypeScript (Astro default), Vercel (hosting), GitHub (source + CI trigger).

**Reference design:** `docs/plans/2026-04-17-portfolio-site-design.md`

---

## Conventions for this plan

- Working directory: `/Users/sammobley/Projects/portfolio`
- Commit after each task using conventional-commit style (`feat:`, `chore:`, `docs:`).
- "Verify" steps use `npm run build` and `npm run dev` + curl/browser checks. No test framework — visual + build-pass is the acceptance bar.
- Never commit `.env` or `node_modules`. Both must be in `.gitignore` before first commit.

---

## Task 0: Baseline Node + package prereqs

**Files:** none yet (environment check)

**Step 1:** Verify Node version

Run: `node --version`
Expected: v18.x or v20.x (Astro 4 requires ≥18.17.1 or ≥20.3). If missing/older, stop and install via `nvm install --lts`.

**Step 2:** Verify npm

Run: `npm --version`
Expected: any 9.x or 10.x.

**Step 3:** No commit (environment only).

---

## Task 1: Scaffold Astro project in existing directory

**Files:**
- Create: `package.json`, `astro.config.mjs`, `tsconfig.json`, `src/**`, `public/**` (via Astro scaffold)

**Step 1:** Scaffold into current directory (already initialized git + contains `docs/`)

Run from `/Users/sammobley/Projects/portfolio`:
```bash
npm create astro@latest . -- --template minimal --typescript strict --install --no-git --yes
```

Flags: `.` = current dir, `--no-git` = don't re-init git (already done), `--yes` = accept defaults, `--install` = run npm install.

**Step 2:** Verify scaffold

Run: `npm run build`
Expected: build succeeds, creates `dist/`.

Run: `ls src/pages`
Expected: `index.astro` exists.

**Step 3:** Commit

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "chore: scaffold Astro minimal template"
```

---

## Task 2: Add Tailwind CSS integration

**Files:**
- Modify: `astro.config.mjs`, `package.json`
- Create: `tailwind.config.mjs`, `src/styles/global.css`

**Step 1:** Install Tailwind via Astro integration

Run: `npx astro add tailwind --yes`
Expected: modifies `astro.config.mjs`, creates `tailwind.config.mjs`, installs `@astrojs/tailwind` + `tailwindcss`.

**Step 2:** Create global stylesheet

Create `src/styles/global.css`:

```css
@tailwind base;
@tailwind components;
@tailwind utilities;

:root {
  color-scheme: light;
}

html {
  font-family: 'Geist', system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif;
  -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
  -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale;
}

code, pre, .mono {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, monospace;
}
```

**Step 3:** Verify build

Run: `npm run build`
Expected: succeeds.

**Step 4:** Commit

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "feat: add Tailwind CSS integration and global styles"
```

---

## Task 3: Add MDX integration + content collections

**Files:**
- Modify: `astro.config.mjs`, `package.json`
- Create: `src/content/config.ts`

**Step 1:** Install MDX

Run: `npx astro add mdx --yes`
Expected: installs `@astrojs/mdx`, adds to `astro.config.mjs`.

**Step 2:** Create content collection config

Create `src/content/config.ts`:

```ts
import { defineCollection, z } from 'astro:content';

const projects = defineCollection({
  type: 'content',
  schema: z.object({
    title: z.string(),
    description: z.string(),
    tech: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
    status: z.enum(['live', 'testflight', 'internal', 'dev']),
    url: z.string().url().optional(),
    testflight: z.string().url().optional(),
    github: z.string().url().optional(),
    featured: z.boolean().default(false),
    order: z.number().default(100),
    published: z.boolean().default(false),
  }),
});

export const collections = { projects };
```

**Step 3:** Verify

Run: `npm run build`
Expected: succeeds (no projects yet, but schema compiles).

**Step 4:** Commit

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "feat: add MDX integration and projects content collection schema"
```

---

## Task 4: Configure Tailwind theme tokens

**Files:**
- Modify: `tailwind.config.mjs`

**Step 1:** Replace `tailwind.config.mjs` with:

```js
/** @type {import('tailwindcss').Config} */
export default {
  content: ['./src/**/*.{astro,html,js,jsx,md,mdx,svelte,ts,tsx,vue}'],
  theme: {
    extend: {
      colors: {
        ink: {
          DEFAULT: '#111111',
          muted: '#555555',
          subtle: '#888888',
        },
        paper: {
          DEFAULT: '#fafaf7',
          raised: '#ffffff',
          border: '#e8e8e3',
        },
        accent: {
          DEFAULT: '#3f6b5e',   // desaturated green
          hover: '#2e5247',
        },
      },
      fontFamily: {
        sans: ['Geist', 'system-ui', '-apple-system', 'Segoe UI', 'sans-serif'],
        mono: ['JetBrains Mono', 'ui-monospace', 'SFMono-Regular', 'Menlo', 'monospace'],
      },
      maxWidth: {
        prose: '720px',
      },
    },
  },
  plugins: [],
};
```

**Step 2:** Verify build

Run: `npm run build`
Expected: succeeds.

**Step 3:** Commit

```bash
git add tailwind.config.mjs
git commit -m "feat: configure Tailwind theme tokens (colors, fonts, prose width)"
```

## Task 6: Build base Layout component with nav + footer

**Files:**
- Create: `src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro`

**Step 1:** Create `src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro`:

```astro
---
import '../styles/global.css';
import Fonts from '../components/Fonts.astro';

interface Props {
  title: string;
  description?: string;
}

const { title, description = 'Sam Mobley — developer portfolio' } = Astro.props;

const nav = [
  { href: '/', label: 'Home' },
  { href: '/projects', label: 'Projects' },
  { href: 'https://medium.com/@sammobley', label: 'Writing', external: true },
  { href: '/about', label: 'About' },
];
---
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8" />
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
    <meta name="generator" content={Astro.generator} />
    <meta name="description" content={description} />
    <title>{title}</title>
    <Fonts />
  </head>
  <body class="bg-paper text-ink min-h-screen antialiased">
    <header class="border-b border-paper-border">
      <nav class="mx-auto max-w-prose flex items-center justify-between px-6 py-5">
        <a href="/" class="font-medium tracking-tight">Sam Mobley</a>
        <ul class="flex gap-6 text-sm">
          {nav.map((item) => (
            <li>
              <a
                href={item.href}
                target={item.external ? '_blank' : undefined}
                rel={item.external ? 'noopener noreferrer' : undefined}
                class="text-ink-muted hover:text-ink transition-colors"
              >
                {item.label}
              </a>
            </li>
          ))}
        </ul>
      </nav>
    </header>
    <main class="mx-auto max-w-prose px-6 py-16">
      <slot />
    </main>
    <footer class="border-t border-paper-border mt-24">
      <div class="mx-auto max-w-prose px-6 py-8 text-sm text-ink-subtle flex justify-between">
        <span>© {new Date().getFullYear()} Sam Mobley</span>
        <span class="flex gap-4">
          <a href="https://github.com/sammobley" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="hover:text-ink">GitHub</a>
          <a href="https://medium.com/@sammobley" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="hover:text-ink">Medium</a>
        </span>
      </div>
    </footer>
  </body>
</html>
```

**Step 2:** Verify

Run: `npm run build`
Expected: succeeds (layout not yet consumed; build still passes).

**Step 3:** Commit

```bash
git add src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro
git commit -m "feat: add BaseLayout with nav and footer"
```

## Task 7: Build ProjectCard component

**Files:**
- Create: `src/components/ProjectCard.astro`

**Step 1:** Create `src/components/ProjectCard.astro`:

```astro
---
import type { CollectionEntry } from 'astro:content';

interface Props {
  project: CollectionEntry<'projects'>;
}

const { project } = Astro.props;
const { title, description, tech, status, url, testflight, github, featured } = project.data;

// Card links to detail page if featured, else to external URL/TestFlight/GitHub.
const primaryHref = featured
  ? `/projects/${project.slug}`
  : url ?? testflight ?? github ?? `/projects/${project.slug}`;

const external = !featured && !!(url || testflight || github);

const statusLabel = {
  live: 'Live',
  testflight: 'TestFlight',
  internal: 'Internal',
  dev: 'In Development',
}[status];
---
<a
  href={primaryHref}
  target={external ? '_blank' : undefined}
  rel={external ? 'noopener noreferrer' : undefined}
  class="block border border-paper-border rounded-md px-5 py-5 bg-paper-raised hover:border-ink transition-colors group"
>
  <div class="flex items-baseline justify-between gap-4">
    <h3 class="text-base font-medium tracking-tight group-hover:text-accent transition-colors">
      {title}
    </h3>
    <span class="text-xs font-mono text-ink-subtle whitespace-nowrap">{statusLabel}</span>
  </div>
  <p class="mt-2 text-sm text-ink-muted leading-relaxed">{description}</p>
  {tech.length > 0 && (
    <ul class="mt-4 flex flex-wrap gap-2">
      {tech.map((t) => (
        <li class="text-xs font-mono text-ink-subtle">{t}</li>
      ))}
    </ul>
  )}
</a>
```

**Step 2:** Verify

Run: `npm run build`
Expected: succeeds.

**Step 3:** Commit

```bash
git add src/components/ProjectCard.astro
git commit -m "feat: add ProjectCard component"
```

## Overview

A personal options trading dashboard that pulls live data from Schwab's API, ranks contracts using a rating model, and surfaces suggestions across multiple timeframes.

## What it does

- Pulls live quotes + chain data from Schwab
- Ranks contracts using a custom scoring model with auto-calibration
- Streams contract updates through a live feed
- Archives sessions for later analysis

## Why I built it

Personal interest in algorithmic trading plus a genuine itch to work with a real-time financial data stream end-to-end.

## Lessons

_To be written._

## Task 9: Build Projects listing page

**Files:**
- Create: `src/pages/projects.astro`

**Step 1:** Create `src/pages/projects.astro`:

```astro
---
import { getCollection } from 'astro:content';
import BaseLayout from '../layouts/BaseLayout.astro';
import ProjectCard from '../components/ProjectCard.astro';

const projects = (await getCollection('projects', ({ data }) => data.published))
  .sort((a, b) => a.data.order - b.data.order);
---
<BaseLayout title="Projects — Sam Mobley" description="Things I've built.">
  <h1 class="text-2xl font-semibold tracking-tight mb-2">Projects</h1>
  <p class="text-ink-muted mb-10">A curated selection of things I've shipped or am actively working on.</p>

  {projects.length === 0 ? (
    <p class="text-ink-subtle text-sm italic">No published projects yet — check back soon.</p>
  ) : (
    <ul class="grid gap-4">
      {projects.map((project) => (
        <li>
          <ProjectCard project={project} />
        </li>
      ))}
    </ul>
  )}
</BaseLayout>
```

**Step 2:** Verify

Run: `npm run build`
Expected: succeeds.

Run: `npm run dev` (in a separate terminal)
Visit: `http://localhost:4321/projects`
Expected: page renders with "No published projects yet" message (all seeded as unpublished).

Stop dev server.

**Step 3:** Commit

```bash
git add src/pages/projects.astro
git commit -m "feat: add /projects listing page"
```

---

## Task 10: Build project detail page (for featured)

**Files:**
- Create: `src/pages/projects/[...slug].astro`

**Step 1:** Create `src/pages/projects/[...slug].astro`:

```astro
---
import { getCollection, type CollectionEntry } from 'astro:content';
import BaseLayout from '../../layouts/BaseLayout.astro';

export async function getStaticPaths() {
  const projects = await getCollection('projects', ({ data }) => data.published && data.featured);
  return projects.map((project) => ({
    params: { slug: project.slug },
    props: { project },
  }));
}

interface Props {
  project: CollectionEntry<'projects'>;
}

const { project } = Astro.props;
const { Content } = await project.render();
const { title, description, tech, url, github, testflight, status } = project.data;

const statusLabel = {
  live: 'Live',
  testflight: 'TestFlight',
  internal: 'Internal',
  dev: 'In Development',
}[status];
---
<BaseLayout title={`${title} — Sam Mobley`} description={description}>
  <article>
    <a href="/projects" class="text-sm text-ink-subtle hover:text-ink">← All projects</a>

    <header class="mt-6 mb-10">
      <div class="flex items-baseline justify-between gap-4 mb-3">
        <h1 class="text-3xl font-semibold tracking-tight">{title}</h1>
        <span class="text-xs font-mono text-ink-subtle whitespace-nowrap">{statusLabel}</span>
      </div>
      <p class="text-ink-muted text-lg">{description}</p>
      {tech.length > 0 && (
        <ul class="mt-4 flex flex-wrap gap-2">
          {tech.map((t) => (
            <li class="text-xs font-mono text-ink-subtle">{t}</li>
          ))}
        </ul>
      )}
      <div class="mt-6 flex gap-4 text-sm">
        {url && <a href={url} target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-accent hover:text-accent-hover">Visit site →</a>}
        {testflight && <a href={testflight} target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-accent hover:text-accent-hover">TestFlight →</a>}
        {github && <a href={github} target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-accent hover:text-accent-hover">GitHub →</a>}
      </div>
    </header>

    <div class="prose prose-neutral max-w-none prose-headings:font-semibold prose-headings:tracking-tight prose-a:text-accent">
      <Content />
    </div>
  </article>
</BaseLayout>
```

**Step 2:** Install Tailwind typography plugin (used above via `prose` classes)

Run: `npm install -D @tailwindcss/typography`

**Step 3:** Register the plugin

Edit `tailwind.config.mjs`:

```js
import typography from '@tailwindcss/typography';

/** @type {import('tailwindcss').Config} */
export default {
  content: ['./src/**/*.{astro,html,js,jsx,md,mdx,svelte,ts,tsx,vue}'],
  theme: { /* unchanged */ },
  plugins: [typography],
};
```

(Preserve the existing `theme.extend` block — only add the `import` at the top and add `typography` to `plugins`.)

**Step 4:** Verify

Run: `npm run build`
Expected: succeeds. Because no project is both `published: true` AND `featured: true`, no detail pages are generated — that's correct.

**Step 5:** Commit

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "feat: add featured-project detail template and typography plugin"
```

## Task 11: Build Home page

**Files:**
- Modify/replace: `src/pages/index.astro`

**Step 1:** Replace `src/pages/index.astro` with:

```astro
---
import { getCollection } from 'astro:content';
import BaseLayout from '../layouts/BaseLayout.astro';
import ProjectCard from '../components/ProjectCard.astro';

const featured = (await getCollection('projects', ({ data }) => data.published && data.featured))
  .sort((a, b) => a.data.order - b.data.order)
  .slice(0, 2);
---
<BaseLayout title="Sam Mobley" description="Developer. Building small, useful things.">
  <section class="mb-20">
    <h1 class="text-3xl font-semibold tracking-tight mb-4">Hi, I'm Sam.</h1>
    <p class="text-ink-muted text-lg leading-relaxed max-w-xl">
      I build small, useful software — web tools, iOS apps, and the occasional trading experiment.
      Most of what I make starts as a personal itch; sometimes they turn into things worth sharing.
    </p>
  </section>

  {featured.length > 0 && (
    <section class="mb-20">
      <div class="flex items-baseline justify-between mb-6">
        <h2 class="text-lg font-medium tracking-tight">Featured</h2>
        <a href="/projects" class="text-sm text-ink-muted hover:text-ink">All projects →</a>
      </div>
      <ul class="grid gap-4">
        {featured.map((project) => (
          <li>
            <ProjectCard project={project} />
          </li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </section>
  )}

  <section>
    <h2 class="text-lg font-medium tracking-tight mb-4">Elsewhere</h2>
    <ul class="space-y-2 text-sm">
      <li>
        <a href="https://medium.com/@sammobley" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-ink-muted hover:text-ink">
          Writing on Medium →
        </a>
      </li>
      <li>
        <a href="https://github.com/sammobley" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-ink-muted hover:text-ink">
          Code on GitHub →
        </a>
      </li>
    </ul>
  </section>
</BaseLayout>
```

**Step 2:** Verify

Run: `npm run build`
Expected: succeeds.

Run: `npm run dev`
Visit: `http://localhost:4321/`
Expected: home page renders with intro, no featured section (nothing featured yet), and "Elsewhere" links. Stop dev server.

**Step 3:** Commit

```bash
git add src/pages/index.astro
git commit -m "feat: add home page with featured projects and outbound links"
```

```astro
---
import BaseLayout from '../layouts/BaseLayout.astro';
---
<BaseLayout title="About — Sam Mobley">
  <h1 class="text-2xl font-semibold tracking-tight mb-6">About</h1>
  <div class="prose prose-neutral max-w-none prose-a:text-accent">
    <p>
      I'm Sam Mobley — a developer building a mix of web tools, iOS apps, and data-heavy dashboards.
      I'm less interested in the "next big thing" and more in shipping small, useful software that
      solves a specific problem well.
    </p>
    <p>
      Tooling I reach for often: Python, Swift/SwiftUI, TypeScript, and whatever static site framework
      gets out of my way. Most of what I build starts as something I wanted for myself.
    </p>
    <p>
      Get in touch: <a href="mailto:sammobley@me.com">sammobley@me.com</a>.
    </p>
  </div>
</BaseLayout>

## Task 13: Add .gitignore, README.md, .env.example

**Files:**
- Create: `.gitignore`, `README.md`, `.env.example`

**Step 1:** Create `.gitignore`:

```
# Dependencies
node_modules/

# Build output
dist/
.astro/

# Environment
.env
.env.local
.env.*.local

# Logs
*.log
npm-debug.log*

# Editor
.vscode/
.idea/
.DS_Store

# Cache
.cache/
```

**Step 2:** Create `README.md`:

```markdown
# portfolio

Sam Mobley's personal portfolio site.

## Stack

- [Astro](https://astro.build) (static site generator)
- Tailwind CSS
- MDX for featured project detail pages
- Deployed to Vercel

## Local development

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
```

Dev server runs at `http://localhost:4321`.

## Adding or editing projects

Each project is a Markdown/MDX file in `src/content/projects/`.

Frontmatter fields:

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `title` | string | Required |
| `description` | string | 1-2 sentence summary |
| `tech` | string[] | Stack badges shown on card |
| `status` | `live` / `testflight` / `internal` / `dev` | Required |
| `url` | URL | Live web link |
| `testflight` | URL | TestFlight join link |
| `github` | URL | Public repo link |
| `featured` | boolean | If true, surfaces on home and gets a detail page |
| `order` | number | Sort on `/projects` (lower first) |
| `published` | boolean | Must be `true` to appear anywhere |

To add a new project:
1. Copy an existing file in `src/content/projects/`
2. Edit frontmatter
3. Set `published: true` when ready
4. Commit + push — auto-deploys via Vercel

## Status

In development.
```

**Step 3:** Create `.env.example`:

```
# No environment variables required at this time.
# Add future keys here (e.g., analytics IDs) and keep actual values in .env (gitignored).
```

**Step 4:** Verify

Run: `git status`
Expected: `.env.example`, `.gitignore`, `README.md` shown as new/modified. No `.env` present.

**Step 5:** Commit

```bash
git add .gitignore README.md .env.example
git commit -m "docs: add README, .gitignore, .env.example"
```

---

## Task 14: Add vercel.json build hints (optional but safe)

**Files:**
- Create: `vercel.json`

**Step 1:** Create `vercel.json`:

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json",
  "framework": "astro",
  "buildCommand": "npm run build",
  "outputDirectory": "dist",
  "installCommand": "npm install"
}
```

This is belt-and-suspenders — Vercel auto-detects Astro, but being explicit avoids surprises.

**Step 2:** Verify

Run: `npm run build`
Expected: succeeds.

**Step 3:** Commit

```bash
git add vercel.json
git commit -m "chore: add vercel.json for explicit build config"
```

---

## Task 15: Create GitHub repo and push

**Files:** none (remote setup)

**Step 1:** Create public GitHub repo

Using gh CLI:

```bash
gh repo create sammobley/portfolio --public --source=. --remote=origin --push
```

If gh not installed, fall back to creating the repo at https://github.com/new (name: `portfolio`, public, no README/license/gitignore — we have our own) then:

```bash
git remote add origin https://github.com/sammobley/portfolio.git
git branch -M main
git push -u origin main
```

**Step 2:** Verify remote + push

Run: `git remote -v`
Expected: shows `origin` pointing at `github.com/sammobley/portfolio`.

Run: `git log --oneline -5`
Expected: commits present locally. Confirm they appear on GitHub in the browser.

**Step 3:** No commit needed — this task is remote setup.

---

## Task 16: Deploy to Vercel

**Files:** none (hosting setup)

**Step 1:** Install Vercel CLI (if not installed)

Run: `npm i -g vercel`

**Step 2:** Link and deploy

Run from project root:

```bash
vercel link
vercel --prod
```

Interactive prompts:
- Scope: personal Vercel account
- Link to existing project? No
- Project name: `portfolio`
- Directory: `./`
- Overrides? No

**Step 3:** Verify production URL

Vercel returns a URL like `portfolio-xxxxx.vercel.app`. Open it, confirm:
- Home page loads
- `/projects` shows "No published projects yet"
- `/about` loads
- Nav works, footer links open Medium + GitHub

**Step 4:** Confirm GitHub auto-deploy linked

Push a trivial commit (e.g., whitespace in README) and confirm Vercel deploys it automatically. Revert if desired.

**Step 5:** No local commit needed.

---

## Task 17: Point custom domain at Vercel

**Files:** none (DNS)

**Step 1:** In Vercel dashboard → project → Settings → Domains, add Sam's custom domain (e.g., `sammobley.com`).

**Step 2:** Vercel will show required DNS records. Typically:
- `A` record for root: `76.76.21.21`
- `CNAME` record for `www`: `cname.vercel-dns.com`

**Step 3:** In Sam's current domain registrar (Bluehost or elsewhere), add/update those DNS records.

**Step 4:** Wait for DNS propagation (5 min – 1 hour). Vercel shows green checkmarks when domain is live.

**Step 5:** Verify with `curl -I https://<domain>` — should return 200 and be served by Vercel.

**Step 6:** No commit needed.

---

## Task 18: Pick featured projects and publish lineup

**Files:**
- Modify: 2 files in `src/content/projects/` (flip `published: true` + `featured: true` on Sam's picks)
- Modify: remaining files Sam wants public (flip `published: true`)

**Step 1:** Sam reviews seeded files and decides which to publish. Suggested starting point (confirm with Sam):
- Featured: Tax Burden Calculator + Options Intelligence
- Published (card-only): World Pulse, Italy Trip, Sign Tracker, Mood Tracker
- Kept hidden: Cycle Nutrition, Project Dashboard, Project OS (until ready)

For each selected project, edit its frontmatter to set `published: true` and, for featured picks, `featured: true`.

**Step 2:** Fill in real URLs / TestFlight links / GitHub links where available. Replace any `REPLACE` placeholders.

**Step 3:** For featured projects, write the MDX body content (overview, what it does, why I built it, lessons).

**Step 4:** Verify locally

Run: `npm run dev`
Visit: `/`, `/projects`, `/projects/tax-burden-calculator` (or chosen featured slugs)
Expected: cards show, detail pages render, status badges and links work.

**Step 5:** Commit + push

```bash
git add src/content/projects
git commit -m "content: publish initial project lineup and featured case studies"
git push
```

Vercel auto-deploys.

---

## Done criteria

- Site reachable at custom domain with TLS (Vercel auto-issues)
- At least 1 project published on `/projects`
- At least 1 featured project with detail page rendering
- Home page shows intro + featured strip (or empty state if Sam chooses not to feature yet)
- Lighthouse score spot-check ≥ 90 on home and `/projects` (run from Chrome DevTools)
- `npm run build` clean in CI (Vercel)
- No secrets committed
